_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Operative repair is indicated for symptomatic occlusion of the proximal arterial supply.

preauth.label: Clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management and advice for admission
Angiogram / CT Angiogram / MRI reports investigations confirming the diagnosis

claim.label: Invoice/barcode of graft used (if artificial graft used)

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management and advice for admission submitted?
Was the Angiogram / CT Angiogram / MRI reports report suggestive of Peripheral arterial diseases submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Are Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) submitted?
Are the detailed Procedure / Operative notes submitted?
Is the Invoice/barcode of graft used submitted? (if artificial graft used)
Is there a Detailed Discharge Summary mentioning date of follow-up submitted?

common_queries: Patient's Angiogram / CT Angiogram / MRI reports not indicative of Peripheral arterial diseases
Clinical notes for pre-authorization missing or incomplete, lacking details on history, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned management, or admission advice
Angiogram / CT Angiogram / MRI reports confirming the diagnosis not submitted or incomplete
Invoice/barcode of graft used missing or not submitted (if an artificial graft was used)
Detailed Discharge Summary missing or incomplete, or not mentioning date of follow-up

clinical_key_pointers: Peripheral artery disease (PAD) occurs when plaque accumulates in the arterial system and obstructs blood flow.

Atherosclerosis plaques accumulates inside the arterial intima and obstructs the lumen of the vessel causing a reduction in blood flow, which leads to diminished oxygen supply to the recipient tissues.
An arterial aneurysm is a bulge in the artery that develops in areas where the arterial wall is weak.
Also, certain natural branch points and curvatures within the vascular tree are more susceptible to atherosclerosis due to turbulent blood flow and shear stress.
Diagnosis can be made via EMG and/or nerve conduction studies, Ultrasound (arterial Duplex scan), CT scan (CTA), or Angiography (DSA).
surgical_options: Treatment plan is usually based on severity.

Interventional procedures include angioplasty (to widen or clear the blocked vessel), angioplasty with stent placement (to support the cleared vessel and keep it open), or atherectomy (to remove the blockage).
